text:
Once upon a time, deep in a forest filled with whispers and shadows, there lived a humble fox named Felix. His fur was as bright as sunbeam, and he had a cunning mind. One day, while collecting berries, he heard strange echoes bouncing off trees. Curious, he followed them.

He stumbled upon an old stone bridge. On it, strange birds perched. Felix watched them. Late afternoon sun made their feathers shine. Then, one bird fluttered upward. It sang a song so sweet, feathers drifted away like autumn leaves.

Felix listened carefully. It was a melody he’d never heard before. He chirped softly. Suddenly, birds flew down. One rested beside him. Felix felt oddly warm. He sang a song of his own kind.

They sang together a while. Friendship grew like morning dew. One day, clouds rolled away. Two strange animals emerged. They were wise old owls.
